color_roles.name: surface_base
color_roles.purpose: primary app background and depth foundation

color_roles.name: brand_interactive
color_roles.purpose: primary interaction and selected state, not decoration

consistency_rules: same component role must carry the same semantic color meaning across screens
dense and full-size variants must not be mixed in one homogeneous list
primary domain action must not be replaced by generic conversion language
component role changes require matching route-level regression evidence

rules.statement: Preserve current route purpose, CTA order, and navigation anchors while improving visual hierarchy.
Use repeated component roles consistently instead of one-off visual treatments.
State, status, and action colors must be semantic and stable across screens.

anti_patterns.statement: Do not turn modernization into a new app concept or unrelated product direction.
Do not rewrite navigation structure unless Graphify/Codex evidence proves the current route contract changed.
Do not invent backend data, domain entities, or unavailable states.
Do not collapse operational product screens into marketing or landing-page composition.

visual_hypothesis_policy.implementation_authority: VibePro spec, current code, screenshots, and gate evidence

consistency_rules: same component role must carry the same semantic color meaning across screens
dense and full-size variants must not be mixed in one homogeneous list
primary domain action must not be replaced by generic conversion language
component role changes require matching route-level regression evidence

rules.statement: Preserve current route purpose, CTA order, and navigation anchors while improving visual hierarchy.
Use repeated component roles consistently instead of one-off visual treatments.
State, status, and action colors must be semantic and stable across screens.

screens.prompt:
Modernize the existing VibePro UI/UX readiness cockpit screen /cli/uiux-prepare using the current screenshot.

Screen intent: existing product screen

Keep the same information structure, CTAs, route purpose, navigation, state behavior, and data dependencies:
INV-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-1: Keep the current /cli/uiux-prepare route, primary user goal, and discovered data dependencies intact.
INV-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-2: Do not remove or rename existing CTAs without an explicit matching implementation change and regression test.

Apply these design constraints:
color roles: surface_base, surface_raised, text_primary, text_muted, brand_interactive, brand, surface, text, success, warning, location, urgency, disabled
component roles: PrimaryAction, FilterControl, ResultCard, StatusBadge, NavigationShell, primary_cta, result_card, status_badge, filter_chip, bottom_sheet, bottom_navigation
CTA priority: primary > secondary > tertiary
state semantics: loading, empty, error, selected, disabled, success, available, limited, unavailable
density policy: preserve-current-density
navigation policy: preserve_route_purpose, preserve_existing_navigation_paths, preserve_back_and_bottom_nav_affordances

Generate 2-4 visual candidates for the same screen. Explore hierarchy, spacing, CTA prominence, state clarity, brand fit, and scanability. Do not create a new app concept, remove dense operational content, invent data, or change navigation.

Forbidden changes:
AP-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-1: Do not implement a net-new app concept, new navigation model, or simplified mock flow that bypasses the current information structure.
AP-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-2: Do not treat any generated design suggestion as source of truth when it conflicts with current code or VibePro invariants.

For each candidate, return design moves, preserved UX, risky or rejected moves, and implementation notes suitable for Codex.
gate_checks: VH-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-INV preserves route purpose, information structure, CTAs, navigation, and data dependencies
VH-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-CTA keeps primary, secondary, and tertiary CTA hierarchy aligned with the DesignConstraintGraph
VH-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-DENSITY improves scanability without reducing required information density
VH-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-STATE keeps semantic states visually distinct
VH-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-IMPL is implementable within discovered files or justified shared components
VH-CLI-UIUX-PREPARE-AP rejects new app concepts, invented data, and navigation rewrites

invariants.statement: Keep the current /cli/uiux-prepare route, primary user goal, and discovered data dependencies intact.
Do not remove or rename existing CTAs without an explicit matching implementation change and regression test.

contracts.statement: Implementation must stay within the discovered files unless Graphify evidence identifies a required shared component.
Visual changes must use the ingested VibePro UI/UX readiness cockpit constraints for color roles, component roles, CTA priority, density, and state colors.

scenarios.statement: A user can complete the same route-level task after modernization as before modernization.

anti_patterns.statement: Do not implement a net-new app concept, new navigation model, or simplified mock flow that bypasses the current information structure.
Do not treat any generated design suggestion as source of truth when it conflicts with current code or VibePro invariants.

design_brief.title: /cli/uiux-prepare design-quality modernization brief
design_brief.body:
Modernize the current VibePro UI/UX readiness cockpit screen for route /cli/uiux-prepare using VibePro's Design Quality DAG.

Preserve the current information structure and UX constraints:
- Route: /cli/uiux-prepare
- Current implementation files: (not discovered)
- Current CTAs: (none discovered)
- Current state names: (none discovered)
- Current data dependencies: (none discovered)

Design quality target:
- Clear visual hierarchy: primary task, secondary task, and metadata are visually distinguishable.
- CTA priority: primary action is obvious; secondary actions do not compete with it.
- State clarity: loading, empty, error, selected, disabled, and success states have distinct visual treatment.
- Information density: keep operational/search density while improving scanability.
- Navigation continuity: preserve route purpose and existing transition paths.
- Component responsibility: repeated UI uses consistent roles and interaction affordances.
- Accessibility: text contrast, target size, focus, and labels are not regressed.

Optional reference system:
- No external design-system bundle is required; infer a coherent product-local system from current code and evidence.

Allowed changes:
- Improve layout polish, spacing, hierarchy, typography, icon usage, state color consistency, and component finish.
- Apply or infer product-local tokens and component roles.

Do not:
- Create a new app idea or replace the current flow.
- Remove existing CTAs or navigation paths.
- Invent backend data, onboarding, or route structure.
- Collapse dense operational information into marketing-style cards.

Return an implementation-ready screen direction with concrete component/layout changes and verification notes.

acceptance: Current screenshot and after screenshot are stored for review
DS drift and UX regression gate checks have explicit pass/fail evidence

checks.statement: Every screen spec has at least one explicit invariant and one explicit anti-pattern.
Every changed screen has before/after screenshot evidence or a needs_setup verification record.
Generated or inferred design output must not introduce a new route, net-new navigation model, or remove discovered CTAs.
Design Quality DAG must preserve information architecture while improving hierarchy, CTA priority, state clarity, density, accessibility, and implementation fit.
Design Modernize plans must resolve top-level Journey context and must not treat machine-derived handoff evidence as a curated product Journey.
Verification must include typecheck/build plus route-level visual review for all screens in this plan.
/cli/uiux-prepare keeps route, current files, CTAs, and data dependencies aligned with Graphify/Codex evidence.
